what_it_does: Stores YAML frontmatter metadata that registers an image attachment for the design-source channel memory, pointing to an image target URI.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment_type of images.
The target is a URI-style reference image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-10.
It has a UUID-style id att-78eeaa2f and an ISO timestamp in the added field.
It belongs to the design-source channel.
standouts: The target uses an image:// URI scheme rather than a filesystem path.
The file is purely a metadata stub with no descriptive text about the image.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata keys: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
File is named att-<uuid>.md under channel-memory/channel_attachments/.
References external media via a channel-scoped URI.
concerns: The image:// target is opaque; this file alone does not confirm the asset exists or is reachable.
There is no caption, alt text, or media properties to describe the image content.
notes: A minimal metadata stub that links the design-source channel memory to pitch-deck image p-10.
questions: What does p-10 refer to within the pitch-deck?
Where is the actual image asset stored relative to the image:// URI?
Which channel-memory entry is this attachment associated with?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an image attachment record, and the file does exactly that with a target URI and metadata.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create an addressable, retrievable record that associates a pitch-deck image with the design-source channel memory.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme to target a media asset instead of a conventional relative file path.
contribution: It contributes a small, structured metadata record that associates a channel memory entry with a specific image asset, supporting lookup and linkage without embedding the media itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML metadata stub named att-78eeaa2f.md in the design-source channel attachments that registers an image attachment pointing to image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-10.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to catalog an image asset from a pitch deck as an attachment in the design-source channel memory.
